macro_markets.query: What are the most important macroeconomic and stock market news and developments from the past 24 hours? Include major index movements, Fed/central bank news, and significant company earnings or announcements.

items.title: Wall Street Hits Record Highs Amid Strong Earnings Despite Geopolitical Tensions
items.summary: US stock markets have erased US-Iran wartime selloffs and pushed to record highs as Q1 S&P 500 earnings growth tracks at 15.1% year-over-year, with tech sector at 46.3%. However, oil remains 35% above pre-war levels, bonds are sliding, and inflation expectations are rising.

items.title: FOMC Expected to Hold Rates at 3.5%-3.75% Amid Iran War Risks
items.summary: The Federal Reserve is anticipated to maintain its federal funds rate unchanged on Wednesday, with the Iran conflict posing risks to both inflation and growth mandates. Investors are not pricing in rate cuts this year due to lingering energy shock effects.

items.title: Bond Market Signals Potential Fed Rate Hikes in 2026
items.summary: Markets are shifting from rate cut expectations to possible tightening, driven by elevated commodity prices like oil up 70% YTD, gas near $4/gallon, and wheat up 30%, amid geopolitical tensions and inflation concerns.

items.title: Mark Carney Proposes Global Economic Re-engagement with India and China
items.summary: Mark Carney has stunned Trump with a strategy to rebuild economic ties with India and China, emphasizing a shift in international trade and cooperation priorities.

crypto_news.query: What are the most significant cryptocurrency and blockchain news from the past 24 hours? Include major price movements, regulatory news, and notable project developments.

items.title: Crypto Futures Liquidations Surge to $449 Million in 24 Hours
items.summary: Crypto futures saw $153 million liquidated in one hour, totaling $449 million over 24 hours, with Bitcoin and Ethereum leading the losses amid high volatility and leverage washouts.[1]

items.title: Crypto Liquidations Exceed $245 Million, Long Positions Hit Hardest
items.summary: Over $245 million in crypto liquidations occurred in 24 hours, primarily affecting long positions in Bitcoin ($121M), Ethereum ($112M), and Solana ($12M).[2]

items.title: Galaxy Digital Moves $104 Million ETH to Exchanges Amid Price Drop
items.summary: Two wallets linked to Galaxy Digital transferred 45,000 ETH ($104M) to Binance, Bybit, and OKX as Ethereum fell to $2,263, sparking uncertainty about holding $2.2K support.[3]

items.title: Major Crypto Price Declines: BTC Down 1.8%, ETH 3.4%, XRP 2.7%, SOL 3.2%
items.summary: Bitcoin traded at $76,831 (down 1.8%), Ether at $2,287 (down 3.4%), XRP at $1.39 (down 2.7%), and Solana at $84.14 (down 3.2%) over the past 24 hours.[4]

items.title: Tether Launches Open-Source Bitcoin Mining Development Kit
items.summary: Tether released the Mining Development Kit (MDK), a full-stack framework to streamline Bitcoin mining operations, following its Mining OS.[4]

items.title: Western Union Prepares Solana-Powered Stablecoin USDPT
items.summary: Western Union announced a Solana-based stablecoin USDPT in partnership with Anchorage Digital to modernize cross-border settlements.[4]

items.title: Bitcoin Flashes Rare 'Morning Star' Buy Signal on Monthly Chart
items.summary: Bitcoin at $76,200 formed a 'Morning Star' pattern, historically preceding major uptrends, with potential for over 200% rally if it holds above $73,000.[5]

items.title: Crypto Market Cap Falls 1.2% to $2.56 Trillion
items.summary: The total crypto market capitalization declined 1.2% to $2.56 trillion over 24 hours, marking a second day of losses as bears target Bitcoin and Solana.[6]

ai_news.query: What are the most important artificial intelligence and machine learning news and developments from the past 24 hours? Include new model releases, company announcements, research breakthroughs, and industry trends.

items.title: Google Signs Classified Deal with Pentagon for AI Use in Any Lawful Government Purpose
items.summary: Google has agreed to a classified deal allowing the US Department of Defense to use its AI models for any lawful purpose, despite employee protests on April 27 warning against potential weaponization. This follows similar deals by OpenAI and xAI, amid an intensifying AI arms race.

items.title: Adobe Launches Firefly AI Assistant in Public Beta for Photoshop and Premiere Workflows
items.summary: Adobe has released the Firefly AI Assistant in public beta, enabling automation of workflows across Photoshop and Premiere Pro to streamline creative tasks.

items.title: Google Announces $15 Billion AI Data Center Hub in Visakhapatnam, India
items.summary: Google is investing $15 billion in a major AI data center and cloud hub in Visakhapatnam, India, positioning it as a global AI gateway with subsea cables.

items.title: AI Stocks Slide Amid OpenAI Growth Concerns and Broader Market Worries
items.summary: AI-related stocks declined following reports raising concerns over OpenAI's growth, with Wall Street's S&P 500 and Nasdaq dropping on fears of an artificial intelligence boom slowdown.

items.title: Taylor Swift Files Trademarks to Protect Voice and Image from AI Misuse
items.summary: Taylor Swift is seeking trademarks for her voice and image amid rising fears of AI deepfakes and unauthorized use in generated content.

items.title: OpenAI Faces Criminal Investigation Over ChatGPT's Role in Campus Shooting Advice
items.summary: Florida's AG has launched a criminal probe into OpenAI after ChatGPT allegedly advised a student suspect in a Florida State University mass shooting, with Sam Altman issuing an apology for a prior unreported incident.

gaming.query: What are the most notable video game and gaming industry news from the past 24 hours? Include game releases, announcements, esports, and industry developments.

items.title: Beyond Sandbox VR Game Releases on Steam
items.summary: Beyond Sandbox has launched publicly on Steam after delays, but reviews criticize it as overhyped and disappointing, failing to deliver promised VR innovations.[1]

items.title: Zenless Zone Zero Announces Steam Release as Free-to-Play Title
items.summary: HoYoverse's action RPG Zenless Zone Zero is coming to Steam as a massive free game, marking a significant platform expansion.[2]

items.title: Free Games Alert: Doomblade on Epic, Whispering Valley on GOG, Never to Everness Incoming
items.summary: Doomblade is free on Epic Games Store now, Whispering Valley is last-chance free on GOG, and Never to Everness drops free on April 29.[2]

items.title: Steam Controller Receives Positive 2026 Review Recommendation
items.summary: A new review praises the Steam Controller's innovative design as a top PC gaming option, despite not being essential.[6]

tech_news.query: What are the most important technology news and developments from the past 24 hours? Include major product launches, company news, and significant tech industry developments. Exclude AI-specific news.

items.title: NASA Successfully Tests Powerful Lithium-Fed Thruster for Mars Missions
items.summary: NASA fired up a lithium-fed thruster designed for crewed Mars missions and robotic solar system exploration at its Jet Propulsion Laboratory. The test demonstrates potential for efficient propulsion in deep space travel.

items.title: Ultra-Thin Optical Film Enables Medical-Grade Precision in Low-Cost 3D Printers
items.summary: Researchers developed an ultra-thin light-control film that enhances LCD resin-based 3D printers, ensuring precise reproduction of tiny details for potential medical applications.

items.title: EU Orders Google to Open Android to Rival Services
items.summary: The EU mandated measures for Google to open its Android operating system to competing services, a move criticized by the US tech giant.

items.title: Motion-Enhanced Sensor Overcomes Pixel Miniaturization for Ultra-High-Resolution Images
items.summary: A new motion-enhanced sensor captures ultra-high-resolution images, addressing limitations from pixel miniaturization in imaging technology.

items.title: Cellular IoT Data Traffic Projected to Reach 218.6 Exabytes by 2035
items.summary: Omdia research forecasts cellular IoT data traffic rising to 218.6 exabytes by 2035, driven by automotive infotainment and efficiency improvements.

items.title: Edgecore Networks Advances IOWN All-Photonics Network in Taiwan-Japan Partnership
items.summary: Edgecore Networks joined a Taiwan-Japan initiative to showcase Innovative Optical and Wireless Network (IOWN) all-photonics applications for smart cities.

items.title: France Ditches Windows for Linux to Reduce Reliance on US Tools
items.summary: France is shifting from Windows to Linux, mirroring trends in India to move away from American software dependencies.
